description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a fabric-covered frame and exposed struts and wires for structural support. The wings are straight and stacked vertically, with the upper wing slightly longer than the lower. The fuselage is slender and open, with a visible cockpit positioned near the front and a prominent engine mounted at the nose. The tail section includes a horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical stabilizers, forming an H-tail design. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, suitable for early aviation operations on grass or dirt runways.
description: A vintage biplane with dual straight wings, open cockpit, exposed struts, and an H-tail design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the open cockpit, nose-mounted engine, and the stacked biplane wings with visible struts.
description_view_1: Front-right view highlighting the dual wings, exposed support wires, and the open cockpit area.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the H-tail configuration, twin vertical stabilizers, and the structure of the tail section.
description_view_3: Rear-right view emphasizing the tail assembly, landing gear, and the overall slender fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane, straight wings, high-wing and low-wing stacked configuration with exposed struts and wires, no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with exposed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit with seating for one or two, no canopy.
